title: Tighten CV with race-purging and use only monotonic calibration for final blending
summary: Your feature space already uses strong race/context interactions and will likely expand with race-lap aggregates, which raises leakage risk if folds mix adjacent laps from the same event.
rationale: Your feature space already uses strong race/context interactions and will likely expand with race-lap aggregates, which raises leakage risk if folds mix adjacent laps from the same event. Separately, scikit-learn notes isotonic calibration can change ROC-AUC via ties, while sigmoid is strictly monotonic and preserves ranking. For this competition, selection errors from optimistic CV may matter more than another hand-crafted feature block.
implementation_hint: Evaluate models under leave-one-race-out or leave-one-race-year-out grouped folds, optionally embargoing nearby laps from the same race when building cross-fitted statistics. Use OOF predictions for any target encoding, race-lap aggregate, or stacked feature. For final blending, prefer logistic/sigmoid calibration or rank-preserving linear/logit blends; avoid isotonic unless it proves harmless on AUC.
expected_effect: May lower local CV slightly but should give more trustworthy model choice and safer leaderboard transfer; monotonic calibration can improve probability quality without scrambling rank order.
risk: Stricter CV can make progress look smaller and may reject genuinely useful high-variance ideas if folds are too coarse.
